popularStory.headline: JAMES, 2ND EARL OF DOUGLAS AND MAR
popularStory.description: JAMES, 2ND EARL OF DOUGLAS AND MAR(C. 1358-1388), married Lady Isabel Stewart, daughter of Robert II. In 1385 he made war on the English with the assistance of a French contingent under John de Vienne …

zh.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglas是Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar的兒子，於1358年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素。他於1371年9月23日與Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1388年8月14日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德奧特波恩享年30歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭洛包爾梅露絲。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglas was born in 1358, in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland as the son of Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. He married Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland on 23 September 1371. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 14 August 1388, in Otterburn, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 30, and was buried in Melrose, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglas est né en 1358, à Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Écosse, fils de Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Il a épousé Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland le 23 septembre 1371. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 août 1388, à Otterburn, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterré à Melrose, Roxburghshire, Écosse.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglasは1358年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス カストルでSir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Marの息子として生まれた。1371年9月23日にIsabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1388年8月14日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドオッテルブルンで30歳で死去し，スコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアメルローゼにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglas(은)는 1358년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스 카스틀에서 Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1371년 9월 23일 Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1388년 8월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 오터번에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 멜로즈에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland em 23 de setembro de 1371. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Sir James Douglas 2nd Earl of Douglas родился в 1358 г. в местности доуглас кастл, ланаркшир, Шотландия. Его отца звали Sir William Douglas 1st Earl of Douglas and Mar. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella Euphame Stewart Princess of Scotland 23 сентября 1371 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 14 августа 1388 г. в местности оттирбурн, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 30 лет и был похоронен в местности мелрос, роксбургшир, Шотландия.
